tasks.description: Public routes have a reusable skeleton component set for section headers, product cards, event cards, contact rows, image slots, and detail panels.
Product and catalog routes use shared skeletons while product rows, product details, catalog metadata, and catalog product selections are loading, and they do not create fake dynamic records.
steps: `rtk npx eslint src/routes/products.tsx src/routes/products.$productId.tsx src/routes/catalog.index.tsx src/routes/catalog.$catalogId.tsx`
`rtk npx prettier --check src/routes/products.tsx src/routes/products.$productId.tsx src/routes/catalog.index.tsx src/routes/catalog.$catalogId.tsx`

tasks.description: Events, B2B, and contact routes keep static section framing visible while remote event rows, catalog-derived images, company settings, contact settings, and FAQs load through shared skeletons.
steps: Dynamic skeletons in these routes contain no fake event, product, company, or FAQ copy

tasks.description: Homepage, brand, Gippy AI, and router pending behavior keep static UI visible and use skeletons only for remote dynamic sections that would otherwise block or fake data.
steps: `rtk npx eslint src/routes/index.tsx src/routes/brand.tsx src/routes/gippy-ai.tsx src/router.tsx src/components/site/LoadingModal.tsx`
`rtk npx prettier --check src/routes/index.tsx src/routes/brand.tsx src/routes/gippy-ai.tsx src/router.tsx src/components/site/LoadingModal.tsx`

tasks.description: The completed implementation passes build and targeted lint, and public dynamic sections follow the skeleton-while-loading then empty-state-after-loading contract.
steps: `rtk npx eslint src/components/site src/routes/products.tsx src/routes/products.$productId.tsx src/routes/catalog.index.tsx src/routes/catalog.$catalogId.tsx src/routes/events.tsx src/routes/b2b.tsx src/routes/contact.tsx src/routes/index.tsx src/routes/brand.tsx src/routes/gippy-ai.tsx`
Manual route check: `/products`, `/catalog`, `/events`, `/b2b`, and `/contact` show static shell immediately and skeletons only for dynamic sections while loading

depends_on: Convert product and catalog dynamic loading to shared skeletons
Convert events, B2B, and contact dynamic loading to shared skeletons
Preserve static shells on remaining public pages

patterns: Static route framing should render immediately
Remote record/list/detail sections should show shared skeletons while loading
Empty states should render only after loading completes with no real data
Skeletons must be presentational and must not encode fake product, event, catalog, contact, or FAQ data
nonGoals: Do not use fallback dynamic data to simulate loaded records
Do not redesign the app into a different router or SSR architecture
Do not add data sources or change public content models
Do not expand admin/editor loading behavior
